instructions.how_to_review: 将你想要发布的活动的 'selected' 字段改为 true
instructions.next_step: 保存文件后运行: npm run generate-post [此文件路径]

events.title: Voyage to Paradiso – The Divine Light Party
events.description_preview: Shine Bright. Float Higher. Welcome to Paradiso—a radiant, immersive escape at sea inspired by Dante...
events.description_detail:
Shine Bright. Float Higher.
Welcome to Paradiso—a radiant, immersive escape at sea inspired by Dante’s Sphere of the Sun, where light, warmth, and collective energy converge.
This Independence Day, step aboard the San Francisco Belle for a first look at Paradiso, the highly anticipated rooftop concept from Dante’s Inferno, debuting ahead of its 2027 Mid-Market opening.
This one-night-only celebration serves as an exclusive first look at Paradiso, the highly anticipated rooftop concept from Dante’s Inferno. Envisioned as the lighter, heavenly counterpart to Inferno (the ground-level supper club), Paradiso channels an elevated, escapist energy, blending the feel of an island getaway, a rooftop party, and a private members’ club.
While Paradiso will eventually live above Inferno, Voyage to Paradiso brings that world to life on the water, transforming The Belle, a multi-level vessel cruising the San Francisco Bay, into a floating preview of the concept.
From golden hour to evening fireworks, experience a sunset cruise across the Bay surrounded by flowing linen, sunlit reflections on the water, tropical cocktails, Jamaican-inspired bites, and music carrying you from deck to deck as day turns to night.
As the sun sets and fireworks light the sky in honor of America’s 250th birthday, the energy builds into a lively, Caribbean inspired event, blending rhythm, elegance, and movement across a multi-level vessel.
This isn’t just a party – it’s a preview of what’s to come.

events.title: WALSHY FIRE from Major Lazer Live in Oakland! July 4th Afro Rave
events.location: 2236 Martin Luther King Junior Way, Oakland, CA 94612
events.description_preview: This 4th of July, Afrobeats Oakland present JA's biggest DJ export, Walshy Fire, for an exclusive da...
events.description_detail:
This 4th of July, Afrobeats Oakland present JA's biggest DJ export, Walshy Fire, for an exclusive dancehall-infused late night rave
WALSHY FIRE (Major Lazer) Live in Oakland! 🇯🇲🔥
This 4th of July holiday weekend, we’re bringing one of the most influential DJs in global music to Oakland for a special late-night caribbean-style rave experience.
Straight from Jamaica, WALSHY FIRE of Major Lazer touches down in the Bay for an exclusive dancehall-infused celebration powered by Dancehall, Soca, Afrobeats and global sounds all night long.
As a member of the world-renowned Major Lazer, Walshy Fire has helped shape the sound of modern dance music across every continent. From massive festival stages to Caribbean carnivals and underground dance floors, his sets are known for unmatched energy, deep musical knowledge, and nonstop vibes.
As always, we'll have 3 rooms of music, going til 4am, featuring our resident Djs - KOKO, Juan G, Goma and Momo plus a special Carribean-themed room hosted by FMLY BZNS and featuring guests DJs Big Rich, Smoke-1, Andreas One and One Lovell.
Refunds up to 3 days before event
2236 Martin Luther King Junior Way

events.title: USS Hornet 4th of July/250th Birthday of USA Celebration!
events.description_preview: Come join us for a fun-filled Steel Beach Party & BBQ aboard the USS Hornet on July 4th with food & ...
events.description_detail:
Come join us for a fun-filled Steel Beach Party & BBQ aboard the USS Hornet on July 4th with food & drink, games, tours, arts & crafts fair.
Spend your 4th of July (10AM to 5PM) in the Bay's best location for the day with music, food & drinks, tours, arts & crafts and more!
Please join the USS Hornet as we celebrate Independence Day with a big Steel Beach BBQ with music, food and beverages!
The ship will be converted into a Steel Beach BBQ Party! We will have music playing (Swingin' Blue Stars, King Tide Players), food and drinks to purchase, tables and chairs, free tours of the Island (normally a $12 charge!), some games for you to play and an arts and crafts fair next to the ship on the pier!
In the Hangar Bay, all the normally accessible areas of the ship will be available for self-touring. You can see the rest of our aircraft and Apollo program artifacts (including our Apollo Block 1 capsule, the Mobile Quarantine Facility and the SeaKing helicopter used in the filming of Apollo 13), and our newest acquisition: An F/A-18C!
On the 2nd Deck you can visit the Torpedo Workshop, Sick Bay, Officer’s Wardroom, exhibits such as African-Americans in the Military, Women in the Military, Air Group 11 and many more!
We also will be closing early enough that you will have time to go to your favorite spot to watch the fireworks. Note: There will be NO Fireworks at the USS Hornet.
Refunds up to 3 days before event

events.title: Fourth of July Pub Crawl & Hot Dog Eating Contest
events.description_preview: San Francisco's Biggest Fourth of July Party. Enjoy Drink Specials, Djs, a Hot Dog Eating Contest, a...
events.description_detail:
San Francisco's Biggest Fourth of July Party. Enjoy Drink Specials, Djs, a Hot Dog Eating Contest, a Pie Eating Contest and more.
The CrawlSF crew is renowned for hosting epic celebrations, and this July 4th promises to be bigger and better than ever. Before heading out to your favorite spot to watch the San Francisco Independence Day Fireworks, start things off early at the best 4th of July Pre-party in the city. The event will include drink specials, Djs, a hot dog eating contest, a pie eating contest, party favors, free admission to all bars and, of course, the infamous Find the Gnome Contest.
On Saturday, July 4, 2026, dive into the patriotic spirit from 2PM to 10PM as the Marina District comes alive with a big dose of patriotic fun. Whether you're gearing up for the spectacular San Francisco fireworks show later that evening, craving a full day of holiday fun or simply want the perfect excuse to rock your stars & stripes outfit around town, this is the best place to celebrate.
Your adventure begins at Rick & Roxy’s (2125 Lombard Street). Check in and grab your wristbands, pub crawl maps and some fun party swag to get the celebration started right. After you are checked in, cruise around The Marina to bars like Westwood, Union Street Ale House, Silver Cloud, White Rabbit, Palm House, The Blue Light, Jaxson and many more. Each stop brings new drink deals, great music and that classic Fourth of July energy. After throwing back a few drinks, head back to Jaxson for the Annual Hot Dog Eating Contest and Pie Eating Contest.
Date & Time: Saturday, July 4 from 2PM to 10PM
Participating Bars: Campus, Jaxson, Westwood, Wizards & Wands, Comet Club, Palm House, White Rabbit, Rockwell, Silver Cloud, Sacred Taco, Union Street Ale House, The Blue Light, Rick & Roxy's & more!
For updated San Francisco Fourth of July Pub Crawl Details, go to july4sf.com.
Can you eat hot dogs like a champ?

events.title: 4th of July at Cavallo Point - In Celebration of America's 250th
events.description_preview: Celebrate at Cavallo Point, offering a BBQ, drinks, live music, and the perfect vantage points for t...
events.description_detail:
Celebrate at Cavallo Point, offering a BBQ, drinks, live music, and the perfect vantage points for the Golden Gate Bridge fireworks!
Few places are better positioned to mark America's 250th birthday than Cavallo Point. In our National Park setting, perched at the foot of the Golden Gate Bridge on the grounds of a former U.S. Army post, this setting has witnessed more than a century of American history. This Fourth of July, history comes alive as fireworks will be launched directly from the Golden Gate Bridge, lighting up the sky above one of the world's most iconic landmarks. There's truly no better place to watch it happen than Cavallo Point.
Join Us for a Full Day of Celebration
The festivities begin in the late afternoon and carry well into the evening. Our Fourth of July BBQ brings together culinary favorites alongside beer, wine, and cocktails (available for purchase until 8pm), all set to a jazz band bringing the party to the Callippe Terrace. On the Parade Ground, where soldiers once drilled and marched, kids are invited to fly kites (included with the purchase of a BBQ ticket) and get their faces painted with HappyCake Face Painting (additional fee). A handful of local artisans will also be set up, including Orchid & Pine and Dolly's Collective, Point Reyes Lavender, Woodbine Bakery, and more.
Throughout the day, historic tours of the property will give guests a deeper look at Cavallo Point's military past, and costumed storytellers will be strolling the grounds to bring the history to life in a way that's as entertaining as it is memorable.
Need a break? Grab a seat inside at Farley, serving its full menu all evening, and enjoy acoustic guitarist and long-time Farley favorite John Vicino performing throughout the evening.
Additional parking is available at the Discovery Museum next door on a first-come, first-served basis. Arrive early.
Refunds up to 7 days before event

events.description_detail:
Celebrate 250 years of American spirit in unforgettable style at the ultimate Independence Day dance celebration.
Celebrate 250 years of American spirit in unforgettable style at the ultimate Independence Day dance celebration. This isn’t just a party—it’s a once-in-a-generation Semiquincentennial (250th Birthday!) experience, reimagined as a dazzling, high-energy White Party. Think elegance, excitement, and a sea of stunning white attire lighting up the night.
Step onto the dance floor wearing white. Move to the pulse of your favorite Top 40 hits, expertly mixed by our top DJ. Whether you come to mingle, dance, or simply soak in the atmosphere, this is your invitation to be part of something historic, vibrant, and fun.
Saturday, July 4, 2026
Sheraton Hotel San Jose Silicon Valley
1801 Barber Lane, Milpitas, CA
Perfectly located at Hwy 880 and Montague Expressway, making it easy for you to join the celebration. Free parking included.
Our holiday events are known to sell out, so securing your spot early is highly recommended.
Early Bird: $20 (last day June 20)

At the Door: $45 (if space allows)
A Quick Note About the Venue
We’re proud to host this event in such a beautiful setting. To help us continue bringing you celebrations like this, please buy at least one drink during the evening.
Dress in white. Bring your energy. Bring your friends. And get ready to celebrate America’s 250th birthday in a way you’ll never forget.

events.title: The Ride Ahead Screening at Ed Roberts Campus
events.description_preview: Join us for a special hybrid screening of Emmy-nominated documentary The Ride Ahead, followed by a y...
events.description_detail:
Join us for a special hybrid screening of Emmy-nominated documentary The Ride Ahead, followed by a youth-led panel and discussion.
The Center for Independent Living (CIL) and Mitsubishi Electric America Foundation (MEAF) invite you to a special hybrid screening event of The Ride Ahead, an Emmy-nominated documentary exploring disability, identity, independence, and adulthood.
The film follows Samuel Habib, a young adult with a disability, as he navigates questions about the future, relationships, community, and what it means to live independently. Through conversations with disability advocates and leaders across the country, The Ride Ahead offers an honest and powerful perspective on disability culture, access, and belonging.
Co-Directors Samuel Habib and Dan Habib will be attending the event. Following the screening, both in-person and virtual attendees will have the opportunity to engage in a youth-led panel discussion and community conversation. Samuel will be on the panel, which will focus on employment issues and be facilitated by Jim LeBrecht, an Executive Producer of The Ride Ahead and one of the filmmakers behind the acclaimed documentary Crip Camp.
The Ride Ahead aired nationally as part of POV on PBS, the acclaimed documentary series showcasing powerful independent stories and diverse perspectives.
Date: Tuesday, June 30, 2026Time: 1:00 PM – 4:00 PM PSTFormat: Hybrid (In-person + Virtual Option)
Ed Roberts Campus3075 Adeline StreetBerkeley, CA 94703
Virtual attendees will receive the Zoom link after registering.
The Center for Independent Living is committed to making this event accessible and inclusive for all participants.
ASL interpretation, live captioning and audio description of the film are available. Access is sponsored by Mitsubishi Electric America Foundation through The Ride Ahead film.
Masks are required for in-person attendees at the Ed Roberts Campus and will be provided at the venue.

events.title: The Altons @ Levitt Pavilion San Jose (FREE)
events.location: East Saint James Street, San Jose, CA 95112
events.description_preview: Levitt San Jose presents: The Altons w/ support from Andre Cruz and the Black Diamond Rhythm Band. 4...
events.description_detail:
Levitt San Jose presents: The Altons w/ support from Andre Cruz and the Black Diamond Rhythm Band. 4:30-8pm. Free. All Ages. Downtown SJ.
Join us Sunday, July 5 for a free concert in St. James Park: The Altons w/ support from Andre Cruz and the Black Diamond Rhythm Band.
Presented in partnership with Universal Grammar and District 4 San Jose Councilmember David Cohen. Food trucks on site: Vero's Little Kitchen and La Marquesita. Beer and wine available.
-------------------------
Is this event ticketed?
This event is 100% free. No tickets required. RSVP on Eventbrite to be notified on updates and news.
Is this show for all ages?All ages welcome.
What do I bring?Blankets and low chairs encouraged. Picnics welcome.
How do I get to the concert?
St. James Park is steps from VTA light rail. Street parking and garages available in downtown San Jose. Check the City of San Jose parking map for options near St. James Street.
Will food be available for purchase?Food and beverages available on site. Food trucks rotate every show. Follow our Instagram @levittsanjose for updates.
Rain or shine, the music stays on! In the event of severe weather, updates will be posted at levittsanjose.org and on our Instagram @levittsanjose.
What is Levitt Pavilion San Jose?
Levitt Pavilion San Jose is a free outdoor concert series bringing world-class artists to St. James Park in downtown San Jose. Every show is free. Every show is all ages.
The 2026 season runs May 24 through September 6, with ten concerts spanning Afrobeat, soul, jazz, Latin music, indie pop, and more. The Altons on July 5 is show six of ten.
More at levittsanjose.org.
East Saint James Street

events.title: Come Party at the Pardee: 4th of July BBQ
events.description_preview: Saturday, JULY 4th 2026 ~ Noon - 3:30 Pardee Home Museum ~ The Perfect Place to Celebrate! The 250th...
events.description_detail:
Saturday, JULY 4th 2026 ~ Noon - 3:30  Pardee Home Museum ~ The Perfect Place to Celebrate!
The 250th anniversary, also called the Semi-quincentennial, commemorates the adoption of the Declaration of Independence in 1776, when the thirteen American colonies declared their freedom from British rule. This historic milestone reflects the founding ideals of liberty, equality, and self-governance, and honors the efforts of the Founding Fathers and early Americans who fought for independence and established the United States as a republic . Governor George Pardee's family home is a fabulous destination for a safe and enjoyable way to celebrate this important holiday.
Music by Frederick Hodges
Vintage Clothing Encouraged
Admission includes BBQ (burgers, hot dogs side dishes, etc.) & Live Music
Adult and Youth (12 and older) : $25;
Reservations: book your ticket on Eventbrite or call Richelle Lieberman: 510-381-1973
Please enter through the gate on Castro Street!
Refunds up to 7 days before event

events.title: 250th 4th of July San Francisco Dinner and Fireworks in Ghirardelli Square
events.location: 900 North Point Street, San Francisco, CA 94109
events.description_preview: Join San Francisco Foodies for a Feast in a private dinning room overlooking Alcatraz from Ghirardel...
events.description_detail:
Join San Francisco Foodies for a Feast in a private dinning room overlooking Alcatraz from Ghirardelli Square
Independence Day, not the movie, is here.
A Historic 250 year anniversary of The United States of America. (echoing fanfare) is upon us, come celebrate with a fantastic four course meal and fireworks. Meet San Francisco Foodies, a small group of 3,700 people dedicated to appreciating San Francisco's Food culture and supporting our restaurants, groceries and other bringers of great meals and experiences.
Fireworks in San Francisco are a humors joke, a promise, a once a year local government Sysphian project, or as I like to say, Hope.
In 2025 the Fireworks were seen, they were wonderful, perhaps perfect. We have video evidence.
When I first moved here, in 2001, my first United States Independence Day (July 4th) in San Francisco; I took my son and boyfriend to Fisherman's Grotto No. 9. There we had an amazing dinner, they sat us at a window overlooking the bay and eventually the fireworks were filling the sky clear and perfectly framed, it was a perfect ending to our touristy exploration of Fisherman's Wharf and the surrounding establishments. Sadly, Fisherman's Grotto No 9 is no longer in San Francisco, we are missing that beloved destination.
Sadder still is that it is rare to see actual July 4th fireworks in San Francisco, fog, smoke, wind. Nature's Mother conjures new tortures for those locals who want to see the night sky light up. Even San Francisco's Symphony seek refuge fifty miles south in Mountain View.
Barring rain, there should be a great fireworks display and we'll have the best seat.
Dinner is a four courses with seafood towers, appetizers, your choice of entree and dessert, a cash bar, huge windows to view the fireworks from indoors and a private patio if you want to stand outside.
A toast at the close of the fireworks, at least one.

events.title: July 4th Sausalito Parade Sail on San Francisco Bay 2026
events.location: Schooner Freda B, -Sausalito Yacht Harbor, Slip 465 Sausalito, CA 94965
events.description_preview: 4th of July 2026 on San Francisco Bay- Morning sail and Sausalito's Annual Parade viewing from the d...
events.description_detail:
4th of July 2026 on San Francisco Bay- Morning sail and Sausalito's Annual Parade viewing from the deck of Schooner Freda B.
Celebrate Independence Day with dowtown views of the Annual Sausalito 4th of July Parade from the water!
On this holiday morning sail, you'll board in Sausalito and start with a tour along its scenic waterfront. It was the proposed site for Hearst's castle, and later the cradle of some of America's most cherished music, including Fleetwood Mac's Rumors and Otis Redding's "Sittin' on the Dock of the Bay" (yes, this Bay!).
“If you are being run out of town, get in front of the crowd and make it look like a parade." Sausalito's Madam, Madam Mayor Sally Stanford
A safe and appropriate way to enjoy Independence Day on SF Bay!
Return to dock to enjoy Sausalito's festivites in Dumphy park and perhaps stay a while to catch the evening fireworks!
Bagels Included in ticket price.
DECK BAR - Cash and Credit accepted
Mimosa's, French Press Coffee, Napa/Sonoma Wines, California Beers and Non- Alcoholic Beverages available for purchase from the deck bar.
Schooner Freda B is a sea kind 80' gaff rigged coastal schooner, making her one of the San Francisco Bay's most comfortable and traditional vessels to sail. Experience the romance of yesteryear with a local, licensed Captain and Professional Crew.
Please plan on arriving early to find parking and the boat.
"Schooner Freda B" is searchable on Google/Apple Maps. That is the location for boarding in Sausalito, and Uber/Lyft can drop off there.
For parking, we recommend the municipal lots surrounding Sausalito Yacht Harbor. Parking cost is per hour, and Parking Lot #3 (searchable on Maps) usually has the most space. "Sausalito Books by the Bay" at 100 Bay Street is a central address for parking.
For the closest parking, take a right at 100 Bay Street and park in spaces 1113-1159 (within "Tour Bus Parking Lot").
Once parked, walk north towards The Spinnaker restaurant.

events.title: Friday Sunset Sail - 4th of July Weekend on San Francisco Bay 2026
events.location: Schooner Freda B, -Sausalito Yacht Harbor, Slip 465 Sausalito, CA 94965
events.description_preview: Kick off Independence Day with a sunset cruise on SF Bay. Step aboard the elegant Schooner Freda B a...
events.description_detail:
Kick off Independence Day with a sunset cruise on SF Bay. Step aboard the elegant Schooner Freda B and enjoy the Bay Area's iconic sights!
Step aboard Schooner Freda B in Sausalito for a Friday Sunset Sail on SF Bay, cruising into Independence Day weekend aboard a traditional tall-ship.
As the day's end paints the sky with brilliant hues, join us in creating unforgettable memories that will light up your heart for years to come.
Sights often include Alcatraz, Angel Island, the Marin Headlands, the Golden Gate Bridge, the Bay Bridge, and the San Francisco Skyline, all while enjoying the sunset!
Schooner Freda B is a sea kind 80' gaff rigged coastal schooner, making her one of the San Francisco Bay's most comfortable and traditional vessels to sail. Experience the romance of yesteryear with a local, licensed Captain and Professional Crew.
Please plan on arriving early to find parking and the boat.
"Schooner Freda B" is searchable on Google/Apple Maps. That is the location for boarding in Sausalito, and Uber/Lyft can drop off there.
For parking, we recommend the municipal lots surrounding Sausalito Yacht Harbor. Parking cost is per hour, and Parking Lot #3 (searchable on Maps) usually has the most space. "Sausalito Books by the Bay" at 100 Bay Street is a central address for parking.
For the closest parking, take a right at 100 Bay Street and park in spaces 1113-1159 (within "Tour Bus Parking Lot").
Once parked, walk north towards The Spinnaker restaurant. You'll soon come across a wooden boardwalk that leads to our dock (Slip 465). Look for the two red masts!
If you are lost, search "Schooner Freda B" on any Maps app. Or, see your ticket confirmation for the "boat phone" number to call.
Sailing on the San Francisco Bay offers ever-changing views and exciting microclimates. Weather can change in a minute. Windbreaker, warm base layer, gloves, hat, scarf, sunglasses, and comfortable shoes are strongly encouraged. NO HIGH HEELS!!
Freda B has a restroom on board.
